mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, consumer spending power contracts sharply, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal broad-based domestic demand expansion, which directly lifts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a positive return frequency below 55% or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of cases during early-cycle phases, or autocorrelation in returns completely dominates the macro signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 235.0, inflation expectations accelerate, forcing markets to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 235.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observed quarters or the correlation between the interaction term and returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit growth, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant relationship with 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering short-term risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances or fails to maintain predictive power during contraction phases.
